cases.title: Run the short interview and write only non-default overrides
cases.user_request: Initialize agent settings for this repo. Ask me a short questionnaire and save the result in .ai/swe.json.

notes: The user wants local-first agent collaboration preferences, not project scaffolding.
The file should stay token-efficient and machine-readable.

artifact_requirements: short interview or equivalent preference capture
static_checks: Uses .ai/swe.json rather than an alternate filename.
Keeps the interview focused on behavior-shaping preferences.
review_questions: Does the response keep the workflow focused on local agent collaboration preferences instead of repo bootstrap?
Does it describe or produce compact omit-defaults JSON rather than a verbose full config?
Does it explain how later skills should apply the precedence rule?

cases.title: Quick mode writes only the version marker and leaves gitignore alone
cases.user_request: Run swe:init --quick here, and do not change .gitignore.

notes: Quick mode should be zero-question and should not materialize the built-in defaults.
The user explicitly said not to change .gitignore.

static_checks: Does not ask interview questions in quick mode.
Does not expand the defaults into the saved file.
review_questions: Does the response keep quick mode zero-question unless an existing file blocks the write?
Does it use only the version marker instead of a fully expanded default object?
Does it leave .gitignore untouched as requested?

cases.title: Protect an existing swe.json from silent replacement
cases.user_request: Set up .ai/swe.json for this repo, but do not lose anything if there is already a file there.

notes: An existing .ai/swe.json may already contain local preferences.
The skill should preserve state until the user explicitly chooses to replace or edit it.

static_checks: Reads the existing file before proposing replacement.
review_questions: Does the response avoid silently replacing an existing valid or malformed preference file?
Does it surface a concrete keep/edit/replace decision instead of assuming overwrite is acceptable?
Does it explain what prevents immediate write-back?

cases.title: Do not trigger on project scaffolding requests
cases.user_request: npm init this repo and scaffold the initial package setup.

notes: The request is about project bootstrap, not local agent-collaboration preferences.
The correct behavior is to decline this skill and redirect.

static_checks: Does not propose .ai/swe.json for a package bootstrap request.
review_questions: Does the response clearly state that this skill is not the right fit for project scaffolding?
Does it avoid broadening the request into a local preference setup anyway?
Does it leave the user with a clear redirect instead of a partial init workflow?
